title: Coding Style and Linting
content: All Python code must follow PEP 8. JavaScript must adhere to the Airbnb style guide. Linting checks are mandatory for all pull requests.

title: Code Review Guidelines
content: Every PR requires at least two approvals. Reviews should focus on logic, security, and performance. Aim to complete reviews within 24 hours.

title: Testing Strategy
content: Unit test coverage must be at least 80%. Integration tests are required for all external service interactions. E2E tests are mandatory for critical paths.

title: Branching and Merging
content: We follow the Trunk-Based Development model. Feature branches should be short-lived (max 3 days). Main branch must always be deployable.

title: Documentation Standards
content: All public APIs must be documented using Swagger/OpenAPI. Readme files are required for every repository. Architecture decisions must be recorded as ADRs.

title: Deployment and Release
content: Continuous Deployment is used for the Dev environment. Production releases occur every Tuesday and Thursday at 10 AM UTC.

title: Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
content: All cloud infrastructure must be provisioned using Terraform. Manual changes in the AWS console are strictly prohibited.

title: Monitoring and Alerting
content: All services must expose Prometheus metrics. High-priority alerts should be routed to PagerDuty and Slack.

title: Post-Mortem Policy
content: A blameless post-mortem is mandatory for all P0 and P1 incidents. Action items must be tracked and completed within 30 days.

title: Tech Debt Management
content: Teams should allocate 20% of every sprint to addressing technical debt and upgrading dependencies.

title: Open Source Participation
content: Contributing to open-source projects is encouraged. Significant contributions on company time require approval from the CTO.

title: Data Modeling and Database
content: Schema changes require a migration script. Noisy neighbor problems in multi-tenant databases must be mitigated at the application level.

title: API Versioning and Breaking Changes
content: APIs must be versioned (e.g., /v1, /v2). Breaking changes require a 6-month deprecation period and migration guide for clients.

title: Containerization and Orchestration
content: All services must be containerized using Docker and orchestrated via Kubernetes. Resource limits must be defined for every pod.

title: Secret Management
content: Secrets must never be stored in code. Use AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault. Rotation of secrets is automated where possible.

title: Accessibility (a11y) Standards
content: Web applications must comply with WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards. Automated accessibility audits are part of the CI pipeline.

title: Logging and Distributed Tracing
content: Logs must be in JSON format. Correlation IDs are mandatory to trace requests across microservices using OpenTelemetry.

title: Load and Stress Testing
content: Critical services must undergo monthly load testing to ensure they can handle 3x peak traffic. Chaos engineering experiments are run in Staging.

title: Cross-Functional Collaboration
content: Engineering squads include members from Product, Design, and QA. Regular 'sync-ups' ensure alignment on roadmap and priorities.

title: Mobile Development Guidelines
content: Mobile apps must support the latest two versions of iOS and Android. Performance benchmarks for low-end devices are mandatory.

title: Hardware Prototyping and Lab
content: Lab equipment must be signed out through the asset management system. Safety training is required for all employees using the 3D printers or soldering stations.

title: Architecture Review Board (ARB)
content: Major architectural changes must be presented to the ARB for feedback and alignment with the long-term technical vision.

title: Service Level Objectives (SLOs)
content: Every service must have defined SLOs and Error Budgets. Burn rate alerts must notify the engineering team before SLOs are breached.

title: Pair Programming and Knowledge Sharing
content: Pair programming is encouraged for complex tasks. Weekly 'Lunch and Learn' sessions facilitate knowledge transfer across teams.

title: External Technical Branding
content: Publishing technical blogs on the company engineering site requires review for tone and technical accuracy.
